title: Avoid pure black for text and backgrounds
description: Pure black (#000000) should not be used for text or backgrounds. Use softer dark tones like #333333 for text and dark gray (#121212 or #212121) for backgrounds to improve readability, reduce eye strain, and create a more professional appearance.

title: Lazy loading required for below-the-fold media
description: Check images and videos that appear below the fold (not visible in the initial viewport). These media elements must use lazy loading such as loading="lazy", lazyload classes, intersection observer loading, or delayed source loading. If any below-the-fold image or video does not use lazy loading, the rule fails.

title: Display breadcrumb navigation
description: Breadcrumbs should be visible near the top of the product page to help users understand site hierarchy, navigate back to categories, and improve usability and SEO. Each breadcrumb should be clickable except the current product.

title: Sticky Add to Cart button
description: A sticky or floating Add to Cart button should remain visible when the main CTA scrolls out of view to reduce friction and keep users focused on the primary conversion goal.

title: Show product ratings near title
description: Product ratings should be visible near the product title. PASS if ANY rating indicator is visible: star icons (★★★★★), numeric rating (4.5/5), review count (203 reviews), Trustpilot widget (Excellent), or any rating badge/widget. Any single indicator is sufficient — do NOT require score + count + clickable link all together. Screenshot detection is the primary source.

title: Use descriptive and clear product titles
description: Product titles should clearly describe the product using relevant attributes such as brand, size, color, and key characteristics. Titles should be concise, specific, and optimized for SEO (under ~65 characters).

title: Use tabs or accordions for product details
description: Product pages should use clickable tabs or accordions (e.g. Description, Reviews, Specifications) to reduce clutter, improve scannability, and make information easier to access.

title: Highlight key benefits near product title
description: A short list of 2–3 key benefits should be displayed near the product title to quickly communicate value and capture attention. Benefits should be specific, impactful, and aligned with key selling points.

title: Include annotations in product images
description: Product images should include annotations or badges highlighting key benefits or differentiators. PASS if any annotation, badge, percentage claim (e.g. -63%), clinical claim (e.g. Dermatologically tested, Clinically proven), or benefit label is visible on or near product images — whether as a DOM element OR baked into the image. Screenshot detection is the primary source.

title: Show thumbnails in product image gallery
description: The product gallery should include visible thumbnails to allow users to quickly browse all images without excessive scrolling.

title: Use square images for consistency
description: All product images should use a 1:1 square aspect ratio to maintain visual consistency, prevent layout shifts, and improve the overall gallery appearance.

title: Show product from multiple angles
description: Product images should display multiple angles and close-ups, and include contextual or scale references where relevant, to reduce uncertainty and increase buyer confidence.

title: Enable swipe or arrows on mobile galleries
description: Product image galleries should support swipe gestures or clear navigation arrows (prev/next buttons). Detection: screenshot-first (look for visible arrow buttons on gallery), then DOM check (slideshow-button--prev/next, swiper-button-prev/next, slider-prev/next, carousel-prev/next, aria-label prev/next, or slider library classes). PASS if screenshot shows navigation arrows OR DOM finds prev/next elements OR slider library (swiper, slick, flickity, splide) detected. FAIL only if both screenshot and DOM show no navigation.

title: Maintain consistent image backgrounds
description: The primary/hero product image should use a clean, professional background (e.g. white or light gray). Lifestyle shots, model images, or before/after result images in additional gallery thumbnails are acceptable and do NOT count as inconsistency. PASS if the main product image has a clean background. FAIL only if the main product image itself has a distracting, cluttered, or random-colored background.

title: Include before-and-after images when applicable
description: For products where results matter, before-and-after images should be included to demonstrate effectiveness and increase trust.

title: Focus on benefits in product descriptions
description: Product descriptions should emphasise how features translate into customer benefits. Detection: screenshot-first (benefit bullets near title/description like 'Fades dark spots', 'Evens skin tone'), then DOM check (benefit keywords: fades, reduces, improves, boosts, radiance, hydrates, corrects, dark spot, etc. — 2+ keywords = PASS). PASS if screenshot shows benefit statements OR DOM finds 2+ benefit keywords. Features/ingredients alone do not cause failure — only fail if zero benefit statements exist anywhere.

title: Include a product comparison section
description: The product page should include a comparison section. PASS if ANY comparison format is visible: feature rows with checkmarks (✓) and crosses (✕), a VS/versus layout, side-by-side comparison cards, 'Top Comparisons' or 'Recent Comparisons' sections, or any comparison grid. Do NOT require strict table format, 2-3 alternatives, or 4+ attributes. Screenshot detection is the primary source.

title: Show customer photos using the product
description: Customer or lifestyle photos showing the product in real use should be featured on the product page. PASS if ANY of the following are present: (1) Lifestyle or model images in the product gallery showing the product being used on a person (e.g. serum applied to face, worn clothing, held product); (2) A review section with customer-uploaded photo thumbnails or images inside review cards; (3) A UGC gallery, 'Community photos', or 'Photos from reviews' section; (4) A verified customer reviews section (Trustpilot, Trusted Shops, Loox, Yotpo) that contains review cards with photos or verified purchase badges alongside text and star ratings. Screenshot detection is the primary source — look at the gallery thumbnails and reviews section. FAIL only if the page has ZERO lifestyle/model/usage images AND zero customer review photos AND zero UGC section of any kind.

title: Add video testimonials
description: Video testimonials from real customers must be included on the product page to increase trust, engagement, and conversion rates. A video testimonial must: 1. Include a visible play button (e.g., play icon overlay, YouTube embed with play button). 2. Be located within customer review cards, testimonial sections, or sections explicitly titled 'Video Testimonials', 'Customer Videos', or similar. 3. Feature real customers speaking about their experience with the product. Product gallery videos, product demos, or promotional videos do NOT count as customer testimonials.

title: Preselect a variant by default
description: Ensure one variant (size, color, flavor, or configuration) is selected by default when the page loads. The selected variant must have a clear visual state such as a highlighted border, background change, or selected styling that distinguishes it from other options. If no variant is selected on page load or if all variant options appear visually identical without a clear selected state, the rule fails.

title: Make the main CTA the most prominent element
description: The primary CTA (Add to Cart/Buy Now) must be the visual focal point. It must be placed 'above the fold' (visible without scrolling), use a high-contrast color against the background (e.g., solid electric blue on white), be larger than secondary buttons, and use clear action-oriented text. Ghost buttons (transparent with thin borders) that blend into the background do NOT meet this requirement.

title: Highlight free shipping threshold near CTA
description: Display the free shipping incentive in the immediate eye-path of the CTA. It must be located within 50-100 pixels of the main 'Add to Cart' button (directly above or below), use persuasive threshold language like 'Add $X more for Free Shipping' or 'Free shipping over $50', and be clear and readable without being more distracting than the main CTA. Shipping information only in header banners or page footer does NOT meet this requirement.

title: Display trust signals near the CTA
description: PASS if ANY payment logos (Visa, Mastercard, PayPal, Amex, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Klarna, Shop Pay, Stripe, Afterpay, Maestro, Discover) OR security/trust badges (SSL, padlock, Secure Checkout, money-back guarantee, 100% Safe, shield) are visible ANYWHERE on the product page — including near the CTA, in the footer, or below the checkout button. Detection uses both DOM analysis (element scan, image src/alt, iframe src URLs) and screenshot visual analysis. PASS if either source detects payment/trust signals. FAIL only if ZERO payment logos or trust badges are detected in both DOM and screenshot. Do NOT require badges to be within a specific pixel distance of the CTA.

title: Quantity / discount check
description: PASS if ANY of the following appear on the product page: Tiered quantity pricing (e.g. '1x item', '2x items', '3x items'); Percentage discount (e.g. 'Save 16%', '20% off'); Price drop (e.g. '€46.10 → €39.18'). FAIL if none of these appear. Ignore coupon codes and free shipping.

title: Display delivery estimate near CTA
description: Ensure a delivery estimate is shown on the product page. CRITERIA: The page must show a delivery date, delivery date range, or countdown/cutoff time anywhere on the page (e.g. 'Get it by Tuesday, Oct 12', 'Order now and get it between Tue, Mar 17 and Wed, Mar 18', 'Order within 2 hours'). No Add to Cart button is required. No proximity to CTA is required. PASS if any delivery date, range, or countdown is visible anywhere on the page. FAIL if no specific delivery date, range, or time estimate is shown anywhere.
